content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807
• 428 J Street
• Sacramento, CA 958 12-0807
(916) 322-5660
• Fax (916) 322-0886
December 11, 1998
René Auguste Chouteau
City Attorney
City of Santa Rosa
100 Santa Rosa Avenue
Post Office Box 1678
Santa Rosa, California 95402-1678
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No A-98-279
Dear Mr. Chouteau:
This letter is in response to your request for advice on behalf of Lucille Foster regarding
the provisions of the Political Reform Act (the “Act”).
1
QUESTIONS
1. Is the Assistance League of Sonoma County (“league”) a source of income to
Ms. Foster, a member of the Cultural Heritage Board (“board”)?
2. As a member of the board of directors of the league, does Ms. Foster have an
economic interest in the real property owned by the league?
CONCLUSIONS
1. No. The league is not a source of income to Ms. Foster.
2. No. Ms. Foster does not have an economic interest in real property owned by the
league.
Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109
-
18995, of the California Code of Regulations.


File No. A-98-279
Page 2
FACTS
The board is established by ordinance of the City of Santa Rosa and consists of seven
members. The board identifies buildings or districts suitable for protection under the Historic
and Cultural Preservation Ordinance, makes recommendations to the city council that a
designation be granted or that a special district be formed, and exercises other duties relating to
the preservation of the cultural heritage of Santa Rosa.
Ms. Lucille Foster is a member of the
board.
Ms. Foster is also a member ofthe league. She is currently serving a one-year
appointment, which expires in May 1999. The league is a nonprofit organization that raises
money to fund the health screening of children, housing assistance for people with health
problems, programs for battered women and rape victims, and scholarship programs. The league
also operates a thrift shop and owns the building in which the thrift shop is located.
Ms. Foster does not currently receive income from the league. She has received
reimbursement for travel expenses from the league, but not within the last three years. Because
she is not the president or vice president of the league, it is unlikely that Ms. Foster would be
asked to do any traveling for the league. In addition, Ms. Foster indicates that she would be
unwilling to do any traveling for the league.
The building that the league owns and conducts its business in is located within the West
End Preservation District and within 300 feet of the Railroad Square Preservation District. It is
within 2,500 feet oftwo other historic districts that have been previously recommended by the
board and approved by the city council. It is probable that a number of questions involving each
of these districts will come to the board for its consideration.
ANALYSIS
L
Introduction ofthe General Rule Regarding Conflict ofInterests
The Act was voted into law by the people ofthe State of California, in part, to ensure that
public officials perform their duties in an impartial manner, free from bias caused by their
financial interests. (Section 81001.) To accomplish this goal, Section 87100 ofthe Act
provides:
“No public official at any level of state or local government shall
make, participate in making or in any way attempt to use his
official position to influence a governmental decision in which he
knows or has reason to know he has a financial interest.”


File No. A-98-279
Page 3
A public official has a financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that
the decision will have a material financial effect, distinguishablefrom its effect on thepublic
generally, on the official, a member ofhis or her immediate family,
2 or on any of six economic
interests delineated in Section 87103 and provided here:
“(a) Any business entity in which the public official has a direct
or indirect investment worth one thousand dollars ($1,000) or
more.
(b) Any real property in which the public official has a direct or
indirect investment worth one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more.
(c) Any source of income, other than gifts and other than loans
by a commercial lending institution in the regular course of
business on terms available to the public without regard to official
status, aggregating two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in
value provided to, received by or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made.
(d) Any business entity in which the public official is a director,
officer, partner, trustee, employee, or holds any position of
management.
(e) Any donor of, or any intermediary or agent for a donor of, a
gift or gifts aggregating two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more
in value provided to, received by, or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is made....”
A.
Ms. Foster is a Public Official
B.
Ms. Foster is Making/Participating in/Influencing a Governmental Decision
Ms. Foster is a public official. (Section 82048.) IfMs. Foster votes on a matter, she is
making a governmental decision.
See Regulations 18702.1-18702.4 for definitions ofwhen a
public official is making, participating in the making, or using his or her official position to
influence a governmental decision.
2 Generally, for decisions that may affect the public official personally or his or her immediate family see
Section 82029 and Regulation 18703.5.
This amount is adjusted for inflation and is currently at $290. As of January 1, 1999, the amount will be
$300.


File No. A-98-279
Page 4
C.
Ms. Foster Does Not Have Any Relevant Economic Interests
1. The league has not been a source ofincome to Mr. Foster during
the last 12 months
Section 87103(c) states that a public official has an economic interest in any source of
income of $250 or more if the income was received within the 12 months prior to the
governmental decision. You have stated that Ms. Foster has not received any moneys from the
league within the last few years. In addition, you should note that the definition of income does
not include reimbursement for travel expenses and per diem received from a bona fide nonprofit
entity exempt from taxation under Section 501(c)(3) ofthe Internal Revenue Code. (Section
82030.) If the league is a 501(c)(3), this exemption may apply with respect to future
reimbursement for travel.
2. Ms. Foster does not have an economic interest in the leaguepursuant to
Section 87103(d) since the league is not a “business entity” under the Act
Pursuant to Section 87103(d), a public official has an economic interest in any business
entity in which the public official is a director. Ms. Foster is a director ofthe league. However,
the Act defines a business entity as a “for profit” entity. (Section 82005.) Since the league is a
nonprofit organization, Ms. Foster does not have an economic interest in the league pursuant to
Section 87103(d).
3. Ms. Foster does not have an economic interest in property owned by the league
Pursuant to Section 87103(a), a public official has an economic interest in any real
property in which the public official has a direct or indirect interest worth one thousand dollars or
more. For purposes of Section 87103, indirect investment or interest means any investment or
interest owned by the spouse or dependent child of a public official, by an agent on behalf of a
public official, or by a business entity or trust in which the official, the official’s agents, spouse,
and dependent children own directly, indirectly, or beneficially a 10 percent interest or greater.
(Section 87103.)
Since Ms. Foster does not own the real property herself and because the league is not a
business entity in which Ms. Foster has a 10 percent ownership interest, Ms. Foster does not have
an economic interest in the real property owned by the league.


File No. A-98-279
Page 5
D.
Conclusion
Because Ms. Foster does not have an economic interest in the league or the league’s real
property, she may participate in decisions affecting the league. However, if Ms. Foster should
receive income from the league in the future, the conclusions in this letter would likely change.
Ifyou have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at
(916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel
By: Marte Castaños
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
SGC:MC:tls
